title: Xanthous Energizing Uruguayan Corn Paella
description: This Uruguayan corn paella is a unique and energizing dish that combines the flavors of nutmeg and binchotan-charcoal with the sweetness of corn, all in a traditional paella pan. It is an egg-free friendly option perfect for any meal.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large paella pan over medium-high heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the corn kernels, smoked paprika, nutmeg, binchotan-charcoal powder, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the water to the paella pan and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the corn is tender.
Remove the paella pan from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the paella hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
